danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprite type and coloration (fire enemies glow, ice enemies shimmer)
danger_signals.meaning: Signals which elemental sword is required to damage this foe; wrong element = no damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Screen-locked chamber, enemy spawn-in with dramatic pause
danger_signals.meaning: Shift to confrontation state; progression blocker ahead

danger_signals.visual_form: Guards, fortified structures, tower architecture in later dungeons

danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard; continuous damage state active

danger_signals.visual_form: Elemental-colored energy barriers (wind, fire, water, thunder variants)
danger_signals.meaning: Requires matching elemental sword charge shot to breach; blocks progression

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinctive shrine/temple architecture, calm music transition
safety_signals.meaning: Safe rest point; NPC counsel and quest progression available

safety_signals.visual_form: Clustered buildings, NPCs walking freely, shopkeeper presence
safety_signals.meaning: Neutral safe zones; shops, healing, information access

safety_signals.visual_form: Clear grass/field areas with monster-free perimeter
safety_signals.meaning: Low-threat transit zone; traversal without immediate combat

safety_signals.visual_form: Unique crystalline chamber with dormancy pod visuals
safety_signals.meaning: Point of origin; narrative safety anchor and respawn concept

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Fortified tower architecture; Draygon's dragon form as ultimate sigil
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Draygonia's Finest Four (Kelbesque, Sabera, Mado, Karmine) with distinct elemental loadouts and recurring encounter patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fortresses, dungeons under imperial control (Goa fortress, Mt. Sabre strongholds), weaponized tower structure

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Individual sage identities (Zebu, Tornel, Asina, Kensu); telepathy spell connection
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: No hostile variants; faction provides support only
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Isolated sanctuaries and meditation chambers; scattered throughout world as safe points

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Player character as living anomaly; Mesia as mysterious woman accomplice
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cryogenic chamber origin; research echoes in post-apocalyptic context

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-apocalyptic medieval regression (100 years after 1997 nuclear war)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Primitive architecture mixed with mutated creature designs; magic-focused aesthetic replacing technology
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs operate within magical framework; survivors communicate through telepathy spells; economy based on elemental swords as power currency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increasing fortress density, fortified dungeons, tower construction visible as progression marker
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Guard presence escalates; boss encounters increase in frequency and difficulty; world-state shift toward imperial control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player sprite gains visual aura/glow matching equipped elemental sword; barrier colors dynamically respond to player weapon
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New areas become traversable; previously immune enemies become vulnerable; puzzle solutions change based on sword equipped

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tower becomes visually prominent; atmospheric shift toward climactic confrontation; final dungeon glow/energy signature
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss encounters mandatory; no bypassing faction representatives; Draygon's true dragon form revealed upon defeat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Village NPCs disappear from normal positions; elder character removed from accessible chamber; visual void in social space
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dog NPC blocks Mt. Sabre North passage until kidnapping event is triggered; invisible collision blocks enforce narrative progression

environmental_storytelling.example: Ice bridge created by Sword of Water across shallow rivers signals world-state where magic substitutes for destroyed technology; sword choice reflects environmental hazard adaptation
Creature morphology and elemental affiliation communicate post-war mutation severity; stronger mutants indicate deeper corruption zones
Four sages (Zebu, Tornel, Asina, Kensu) positioned across world map visually represent knowledge network preserving pre-war civilization; their locations mark information hubs
Goa fortress and Mt. Sabre strongholds grow increasingly imposing; visual density of imperial architecture communicates Draygon's consolidating power
Protagonist's amnesia and frozen origin visually establish him as displaced pre-war agent; Mezame Shrine's unique crystalline design signals temporal dislocation
Guards and plot-point NPCs physically obstruct passage; pushing mechanic (directional knockback relative to facing) communicates faction authority over space

entity_behavioral_tells.state: False form encountered first; true dragon form revealed after defeat
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: False self acts as gatekeeper; true form manifests only when false barrier overcome, signaling progression through deception layers

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Each encountered twice; distinct elemental weaknesses
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: First encounters block progression; second encounters return at higher difficulty; pattern communicates escalating threat as faction consolidates

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Scattered across world; accessible via telepathy
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Provide counsel and quest progression; absence from normal dialogue signals player readiness for next milestone; GBC version explicitly ties each sage to one elemental sword creation
Persistent ally throughout narrative; mysterious presence signals deeper pre-war scientific context; her companionship anchors amnesia recovery arc
Normal: collapse animation after each hit, slowing combat; paralyzed: no collapse, no knockback, enabling rapid elimination—behavioral shift indicates status effect mastery requirement
Barriers persist until matching elemental sword charge shot connects; persistent obstruction communicates puzzle-lock state; dissipation signals progression unlock

notes: Crystalis communicates world-state primarily through elemental sword mechanics as both narrative device and environmental lock system. The Draygonian Empire's visual presence escalates throughout game via fortress architecture and boss encounter frequency, signaling faction power consolidation. Sage sanctuaries provide visual counterbalance as safe knowledge nodes. The protagonist's cryogenic amnesia is the core narrative vector—environmental storytelling relies on players learning that post-apocalyptic magic-based world replaced pre-war technology. Behavioral tells (NPC blocking, elemental weakness, sage accessibility) function as progression gates. The four elemental swords themselves are the primary visual manifestation system—sword glow, matching barrier colors, and enemy vulnerability patterns create a coherent communication language for world-state and danger signaling.
